name: Apple Danish Pastries
recipeInstructions:
1. For dry yeast, use water between 105°F and 115°F. For compressed yeast, use lukewarm water between 80°F and 90°F. Sprinkle yeast or crumbled cake into water. Let stand a few minutes, then stir until dissolved.
2. Pour hot milk over 1/3 cup sugar, salt, and 1/3 cup butter. Cool to lukewarm.
3. Add yeast mixture, lemon extract, and eggs to the milk mixture. Stir in all-purpose flour gradually.
4. Place dough in a greased 9x13x2-inch pan. Chill for 1 to 2 hours.
5. Turn chilled dough onto a floured surface. Roll into a rectangle measuring 16x12 inches.
6. Spread 1/3 cup butter over two-thirds of the dough. Fold the unspread portion over half the covered portion. Fold the third section over the first two.
7. Roll dough back to its original size and repeat the folding process twice using remaining butter. Return to refrigerator and chill overnight.
8. Next day, divide dough into two halves. Roll each half into a rectangle measuring 14x9 inches.
9. Cut rectangles into strips measuring 14x3 inches. Twist each strip to form a spiral roll.
10. Place apple slices in the center of each roll. Sprinkle with cinnamon mixed with 1/2 cup sugar.
11. Cover and let rise in a warm place, away from drafts, until doubled in size.
12. Bake at preheated oven temperature of 375°F for about 12 minutes. Makes about 2 dozen.
